Part B.key_evidence: Central source coincident with a PS1 point source (distpsnr1=0.32", sgscore1=0.98), indicating a star. ndethist=59 over deltajd~22d with ncovhist=1601 shows many repeat detections at this position. isdiffpos=f => source fainter than reference in g-band. Reference shows a clear stellar PSF at center.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Stellar variable: the counterpart is a high-confidence PS1 star with small chi/sharp values and the alert represents a negative-flux residual (dimming) at the star's location, with many historical detections consistent with repeated variability rather than a one-off transient.
Part B.alternative_analysis: An SN is disfavored because the counterpart is stellar (sgscore1=0.98) with no galaxy host visible. AGN is unlikely given the strong stellar classification and lack of a resolved host.
